statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will contain two lines: the size of the array and the array elements separated by a space.
statement.outputFormat: Output the maximum sum of a Subarray of size k.

examples.explanation: Subarray sum of size 2 from 20 to 30 is 50, of size 3 from 10 to 40 is 60.
Subarray sum of size 2 from 2 to 3 is 5

hints.text: Check if the memoization is being applied correctly

editorial.title: Incorrect Memoization in Subarray Sum
editorial.intuition: Memoization can be used to store previous results to avoid redundant subproblems.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach involves finding Sum of subarray of size k for every possible subarray.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a sliding window approach and dynamic programming to store subproblems
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a Map to store the sum of the subarray of size k and iterate over the array using a sliding window approach.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Memoization ensures that the same subproblem is solved only once, thus reducing the time complexity.
editorial.dryRun: For the first test case, the sum of the subarray of size 2 is 10+20+30+40=100 but we can select the subarray [10, 20, 30, 40] which will give us a sum of 100.
commonMistakes: Using wrong data structure for memoization

functionSignature.python: int solve(self, k, a: List[int]) -> int:
